newsSince: No new news since June 9. The standout fiscal Q2 print (revenue $974M +17%, net income +56% to $109.9M, backlog ~$3.9B +62%, Communications +35% on data-center demand, Gulf Island acquisition) was reported in early May and was already reflected in the June 8 prior case.

sources.label: IES Holdings Q2 FY26 press release (SEC)

verdicts.rankRationale: Latent AI-node lens: data-center electrical contracting is a real bottleneck and the +62% backlog / Comms +35% print is the basket's strongest demand signal, but it pre-dates June 9 and is largely priced. Slight +1 for backlog durability vs peers; not enough to move rank. Hold 72.
verdicts.bull: $3.9B backlog (+62%) and Comms +35% give multi-year data-center electrical visibility; net income +56%.
verdicts.neutral: Strong but already-known print; +81% YTD reflects much of it; AI link is via data-center construction, not silicon.
verdicts.bear: Contractor margins are project-cyclical; backlog conversion risk; crowded data-center trade.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Continued data-center electrical backlog growth and conversion
verdicts.primaryRisk: Data-center capex digestion / project margin slippage
